response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and backend engineer, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project — nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no evidence of Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain knowledge, no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no mention of Windows platform development at any point in a 15+ year career that has been entirely web, mobile, and distributed-systems oriented.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps practices across multiple roles (GitLab, Docker, trunk-based development)
Java development background satisfies one of the optional desirable skills
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record evidenced by public talks and multi-day workshops
concerns: C++ experience totals roughly six months (National Instruments, 2014–2015) — critically short of the mandatory 5–10 year requirement on Windows
Zero exposure to Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking systems
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; listed SQL skills are MySQL/general SQL without enterprise RDBMS depth required for capital-markets systems
